resume.rawText:
Christopher Gross

West Linn, OR
chris@dreem.one
 | (503) 310-5433
Authorized to work in the U.S. | Willing to relocate

Designer & Energy Consultant

Transitioning into UX, Product, and Systems-Focused Roles

Professional Summary

Designer and energy consultant who thrives in collaborative, high-trust environments and complex decision-making situations. Generated $850,000+ in solar contracts within six months through consultative, relationship-driven sales. Founded and scaled a product brand to $2.5M in first-year Amazon sales by aligning product development, customer insight, and execution systems. Known for steady negotiation, clear communication, and building repeatable workflows that reduce chaos and improve outcomes.

Work Experience
Energy Consultant

Sunward Power — Portland, OR
March 2024 – Present

Generated $850,000+ in signed commercial and residential solar contracts within six months.

Conducted energy assessments and feasibility studies for churches, nonprofits, and commercial buildings.

Guided clients through incentive programs including Energy Trust of Oregon, federal tax credits, and grants.

Collaborated with engineering and design teams to develop customized renewable energy systems.

Built an internal peak-shaving calculator app to model demand scenarios and support faster, clearer proposals.

Sales Designer

Reborn Home Solutions — Portland, OR
April 2023 – February 2024

Top regional performer for three consecutive months; maintained 40% close rate and $150K+ monthly revenue.

Designed custom kitchen and bath remodels aligned with client goals and budgets.

Managed projects from design through installation, ensuring client satisfaction and on-time delivery.

Founder / Owner

Dreem LLC — West Linn, OR
November 2015 – February 2024

Founded and scaled an e-commerce brand, generating $2.5M in first-year Amazon sales.

Led product development, supplier sourcing, photography, copywriting, and marketing strategy.

Negotiated supplier contracts and secured capital for product launches.

Built internal systems for forecasting, inventory planning, and customer experience management.

Textbook Reseller

Green World Books — Salem, OR
September 2005 – June 2015

Achieved highest statewide sales volume for five consecutive years.

Trained and managed top-performing buyers while maintaining strong institutional relationships.

Home Remodeler

Gracer West Interiors — Salem, OR
August 2003 – August 2005

Supported full residential remodel projects with focus on craftsmanship and client communication.

Projects (Product + App Development)

Peak Shaving Calculator (Sunward Power) — Built an internal tool to model peak demand / load scenarios and support faster decision-making in commercial proposal workflows.

Workflow Apps (In Progress) — Building two additional internal apps with AI-assisted development focused on automating repetitive work and improving team clarity and throughput. (Code available on GitHub.)

Once I can actually access the repos that contain these apps, I’ll replace “internal tool / in progress” with clean, specific bullets: what each app does, who uses it, and the stack.

Education

Associate of Arts (Minor in Arts & Music)
Portland Community College — Portland, OR

Skills

Design & UX: UI Design, UX Research, Wireframing, Branding, Adobe Photoshop & Illustrator, Web Design, Photography, Copywriting
Energy & Consulting: Commercial Solar Sales, Feasibility Studies, Incentive Programs (Energy Trust of Oregon), Proposal Development, Client Consultation
Business & Operations: Product Development, Negotiation, Project Management, Market Research, Salesforce (CRM), Business Development
App Development (practical): GitHub (projects available upon request)

Portfolio

dreem.one — UX, visual design, product work, and solar proposal examples available upon request.

1.question: In your most recent role, what part of the work felt most like you?
1.answer: design, creating systems, making sop's. building web apps/tools Making pitch decks, call scripts, data miners and customer proposal slide presentations. Creating automated workflows and sharing great tools with coworkers. Marketing and strategy sessions.

2.question: What part of the role drained you fastest?
2.answer: endless sales cycles, quota driven incentive, jumping from one task to another without real depth or clarity, overall chaos in the company being short staffed and small.

3.question: What do others come to you for that isn’t necessarily in your job description?
3.answer: design, creating systems, making sop's. Making pitch decks, call scripts, data miners and customer proposal slide presentations. Creating automated workflows and sharing great tools with coworkers. Marketing and strategy sessions.

4.question: What type of challenge feels exciting rather than overwhelming?
4.answer: problem solving, product development, customer needs and finding gaps in the market.

5.question: Outside of work, what do you spend time practicing, improving, or maintaining? 2–4 bullets.
5.answer: songwriting , budling tools, making graphics, interior design

synthesis.patternSummary:
You don't just do the work — you set operating rules.

When something isn't working, you don't force it — you tighten constraints.

You notice, clarify, and sequence.
operateBest: Work where measures and incentives are consistent.
Work with explicit scope, ownership, and a stable cadence.
Work where dependencies can be made explicit and handoffs are defined.
loseEnergy: Work where cadence is missing and constraints reset each cycle.
Work where ownership is unclear and decisions have no owner.
Work where scope is unbounded and priorities shift without constraints.

job.rawText:
About the job
Job Requisition ID #

26WD95032

Senior Product Manager, Fusion Platform Organization

Location: United States (Portland, OR; Denver, CO; Boston, MA – hybrid or flexible, additional locations may be added)

Overview

Autodesk is seeking a Senior Product Manager to join the PDMS Platform Organization, where we build the foundational platform and application frameworks that power Autodesk’s product ecosystem. This role sits at the intersection of platform strategy and execution, enabling Autodesk product teams to build scalable, high-quality, and consistent product experiences across industries.

As a Senior Product Manager, you will own a major area of the PDMS platform and product application frameworks, shaping multi-year strategy while driving near- and mid-term delivery. You will work closely with platform engineering, architecture, and downstream product teams to ensure the platform evolves to serve real product needs—balancing technical constraints, business priorities, and customer outcomes.

This role requires strong cross-functional leadership, comfort operating at executive levels, and a pragmatic mindset focused on enabling delivery at scale.

Responsibilities:

Platform Strategy & Ownership

Own a major area of the PDMS platform and product application frameworks, with clear accountability for outcomes, adoption, and impact.
Define and maintain a multi-year product strategy and roadmap aligned with Autodesk’s broader platform and business objectives.
Translate long-term strategy into actionable plans that balance platform evolution with immediate delivery needs.
Clearly articulate the value proposition, boundaries, and intended usage of platform capabilities for internal and external stakeholders. 

Delivery Enablement

Partner closely with engineering to ensure platform capabilities are designed, prioritized, and delivered in a way that enables downstream product teams to move faster and with higher quality.
Work with internal product teams to understand real-world use cases, constraints, and integration needs, and reflect those insights in platform decisions.
Drive alignment across teams to reduce fragmentation, duplication, and friction in application development.
Monitor platform adoption and usage, using data and feedback to guide iteration and investment. 

Cross-Functional & Executive Collaboration

Collaborate with platform engineering, architecture, developer experience, research, and downstream product teams across Autodesk.
Lead cross-organization alignment on platform priorities, tradeoffs, and sequencing.
Prepare and deliver clear, compelling executive-level communication, including strategy presentations, roadmap reviews, and decision proposals.
Act as a trusted partner to senior leaders by framing complex platform topics in business and customer terms. 

Customer & Ecosystem Perspective

Balance the needs of internal Autodesk product teams with those of external developers and partners, where applicable.
Advocate for platform consistency, scalability, and extensibility across a large and diverse product ecosystem.
Incorporate industry context (e.g., CAD/CAM/MFG workflows) into platform decision-making where it adds value.

Qualifications Required

5 + years of product management experience, with experience owning platforms, frameworks, or shared servicesin a large-scale product ecosystem.
Proven ability to define and execute multi-year product strategies while delivering incremental value.
Experience working closely with engineering teams on technically complex products, with moderate technical depth (able to reason about APIs, architectures, and tradeoffs without being hands-on).
Strong cross-functional leadership skills, including influencing without authority.
Demonstrated experience communicating with and presenting to executive audiences.
Track record of enabling other teams to deliver successfully through platform investments. 

Preferred

Experience with large-scale product ecosystems supporting multiple products or lines of business.
Familiarity with CAD, CAM, or Manufacturing domains.
Experience building or evolving internal developer platforms, application frameworks, or shared infrastructure.

What Success Looks Like

Product teams across Autodesk adopt and rely on the PDMS platform to deliver faster, with fewer workarounds.
Platform strategy is clearly understood, aligned, and supported by engineering and leadership.
The platform roadmap balances long-term architectural health with near-term delivery needs.
Executives trust you to frame platform decisions clearly and guide tradeoffs effectively.

About Autodesk:

Autodesk empowers innovators everywhere to design and make a better world. From sustainable buildings and infrastructure to advanced manufacturing and media, Autodesk’s platform enables our customers to solve complex problems at scale.

Learn More

About Autodesk

Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made.

We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world.

When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us!

Benefits

From health and financial benefits to time away and everyday wellness, we give Autodeskers the best, so they can do their best work. Learn more about our benefits in the U.S. by visiting https://benefits.autodesk.com/

Salary transparency

Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. For U.S.-based roles, we expect a starting base salary between $134,000 and $216,700. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience and geographic location, and may exceed this range. In addition to base salaries, our compensation package may include annual cash bonuses, commissions for sales roles, stock grants, and a comprehensive benefits package.

Equal Employment Opportunity 

At Autodesk, we're building a diverse workplace and an inclusive culture to give more people the chance to imagine, design, and make a better world. Autodesk is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and considers all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status or any other legally protected characteristic. We also consider for employment all qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with applicable law.

Diversity & Belonging

We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/diversity-and-belonging

Are you an existing contractor or consultant with Autodesk? 

Please search for open jobs and apply internally (not on this external site).

alignment.explanation: Structural fit shows 2 severe contradiction(s), 4 mild tension(s), 0 signal anchor(s), and 0 skill anchor(s) across 6 dimensions.
supports_fit: Structural Maturity: closest area of alignment — near role demands.
stretch_factors: Authority Scope: significant gap — role demands exceed demonstrated level.
Revenue Orientation: mild gap — role asks for slightly more than demonstrated.
Role Ambiguity: mild gap — role asks for slightly more than demonstrated.
Breadth vs Depth: mild gap — role asks for slightly more than demonstrated.
Stakeholder Density: significant gap — role demands exceed demonstrated level.
alignment.bottom_line_2s: 1 area(s) of alignment, but 2 significant gap(s) require growth beyond your current pattern.

skillMatch.explanation: New terrain: role demands exceed experience in at least one dimension by two or more levels.

stretchLoad.explanation: Extreme adaptation load. Structural mismatch across core authority or scope dimensions.
